title: Expose resource planner data via API

posts.author: DavidDvorak (David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

I want to be able to retrieve resource planner data via API. Currently there is no API dedicated to resource planner data, it can only be pulled as generic board items. I need to be able to see :

  • Is the allocation an assigned resource or placeholder?
  • What is the start/end date of the allocation?
  • What is the allocation rate for that time period?

What are you trying to achieve

I want to build reporting on resource allocation data. The only way to view resource planner data is through a capacity manager object, and stand-alone capacity manager objects have a limit of 50 connected resource planners. There are no dashboard widgets that work with resource planner data. I want to unlock the value of my resource allocation data and have access to it via API so I can pull it into our data warehouse and build more robust reporting.

posts.author: Michaleiba (Michal Leiba)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, I am Michal from the spot-nik team (Do you know of our best selling apps SuperForm and SuperMail?). We just launched a new app- SuperPlanner. Once the resource planning is done in monday, there will no longer be the need for the connection and all the limitations you have today.

Let me know what you think

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @DavidDvorak,

This is a great feature request, and we’ve run into the same limitation.

Since Resource Planner data isn’t currently available via a dedicated API, we worked around this by tracking forecasted and spent hours directly on boards/subitems, tagging responsible users there, and then pulling that data via the API for reporting. It’s not a full replacement for Resource Planner allocations, but it does enable robust external reporting until native API support is available.

posts.author: znydev (Zanovoy Development)
posts.cookedHtml: @YOGESHP Hello, we are encountering the same limitation. If resource is being tracked by board, how do you mimic the resource planning functionality of considering the allocated hours per person across multiple projects in a workspace or from multiple boards?

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @znydev, Welcome to the community!

Since we operate at a regional level, we use regional dashboards that have all relevant boards connected to them. Each dashboard is designed to show people from a specific region, while still pulling in items from multiple regions.

We achieve this by using regional and other details (populated from our central resource directory board when a person is tagged) to filter the dashboards. This ensures that each regional dashboard only displays resources belonging to that region, even though the work items they are assigned to may span across different regions.

We use the workload widget on these dashboards to visualize each person’s allocation. This way, even if someone is working on projects across regions, their workload is still accurately captured and managed within their respective regional dashboard.

This setup allows us to maintain clear visibility of resource workload at a regional level while supporting cross-region project assignments.

Let me know if you have any questions!

posts.author: znydev (Zanovoy Development)
posts.cookedHtml:
Appreciate the welcome and detailed response!

I’ve been working through how to structure multi-level / connected dashboard connections and wanted to clarify if I’m approaching this the right way. Here’s my current setup:

At the top level, I have an “All Projects” board. When a new item is created (let’s call it Project A), I trigger an automation that:

  1. Creates a dedicated PM Board – Project A (with Hours and Persons columns)

  2. Creates a corresponding item in the Regional Board

This successfully links Project A to the Regional Board, so the hierarchy is partially in place.

Where I’m running into challenges is with the roll-up. My goal is to surface a summary of Hours and Persons from the PM Board – Project A back into the All Projects board, but I’m not sure how best to establish that connection or aggregation across boards.

I know this is very technical but would appreciate any guidance on the recommended approach!

Current Behavior:

All Projects Dashboard
└── Project A (item)

Regional Board
└── Project A (item)

PM Board - Project A  (has hours & persons) 
// Incorrect, no link to All & Regional Boards

Expected Behavior:

All Projects Dashboard
└── Project A (item) | Total Hours | People

Regional Board
└── Project A (item) | Total Hours | People

PM Board - Project A  (has hours & persons)
